skill.name: verging-ai-chat
skill.description: Use the verging.ai chat API for GPT-4o text generation with streaming and non-streaming request examples.
skill.summary: Chat completion reference for verging.ai GPT-4o requests.
skill.author: verging-ai

security_audit.summary: AI review confirms the network and API-key handling findings as real but expected for a chat API reference skill. The external command, weak cryptography, and critical combination alerts are false positives from Markdown examples and documentation text, with no executable skill code or prompt-injection text found in the scanned files.

medium_findings.title: Documented Third-Party API Key Transmission
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to send a verging.ai API key in Authorization headers to the verging.ai chat endpoint. This is expected for the service, but prompts and credentials leave the local environment and users must trust the proxy provider.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The files directly document an API key and HTTPS endpoint for chat requests. The behavior is legitimate for this skill, but the privacy and credential exposure risk is real.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Misclassified as Code Execution
low_findings.description: The static Ruby backtick execution findings occur inside Markdown examples and inline formatting. They are not executable skill code, although users may manually run npx or curl commands from the documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is fenced Markdown or inline documentation, not a script loaded by the skill. Manual command execution remains a minor user-safety consideration.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts map to prose, model names, tables, and response examples. No evidence found of cryptographic code, hash use, or security-sensitive algorithm selection.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed lines are API documentation and examples only. There is no call to a cryptographic library or implementation of a weak algorithm.

low_findings.title: Critical Combination Heuristic Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The critical heuristic combines documentation-only command examples, hardcoded URLs, and API-key examples. No evidence found of obfuscation, hidden runtime behavior, data exfiltration beyond the documented chat API, or prompt-injection instructions.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious signals are present only as user-facing documentation. The files contain no executable implementation and no instructions to bypass security review.

content.value_statement: Teams need a clear reference for calling a GPT-4o chat endpoint through verging.ai. This skill explains authentication, request parameters, streaming behavior, responses, credits, and common errors.
actual_capabilities: Documents the verging.ai chat endpoint and required Authorization header.
Shows non-streaming and Server-Sent Events streaming request examples.
Lists supported message roles, model, temperature, max_tokens, and stream parameters.
Explains response fields for content, token usage, credits, and stream completion.
Summarizes credit requirements, token rates, and common HTTP error codes.
limitations: It is documentation only and does not include an executable client library.
It requires a verging.ai account, API key, and sufficient credits.
Prompts and responses are sent through the verging.ai service.
It does not verify current service availability, pricing, or model changes.

use_cases.title: Build a Chat API Prototype
use_cases.description: Use the request examples to connect an application prototype to verging.ai chat completions.

use_cases.title: Test Streaming Responses
use_cases.description: Compare standard responses with Server-Sent Events output before choosing a product experience.

use_cases.title: Document Integration Requirements
use_cases.description: Capture authentication, credit, response, and error behavior for an internal integration guide.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Basic Request
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me create a simple verging.ai chat request for one user message. Explain each required field in plain language.

prompt_templates.title: Add Generation Controls
prompt_templates.prompt: Show how to configure temperature and max_tokens for a verging.ai chat request. Recommend values for concise factual answers.

prompt_templates.title: Plan Streaming Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Explain how my app should handle verging.ai Server-Sent Events responses from first chunk through final usage details.

prompt_templates.title: Review Production Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Review my planned verging.ai chat integration for authentication, credit handling, rate limits, retries, logging, and secret management.

output_examples.input: A developer asks how to make the first verging.ai chat request.
output_examples.output: The response identifies the endpoint, required API key header, message format, and a minimal request flow without exposing a real secret.

output_examples.input: A product engineer asks whether to use streaming.
output_examples.output: The response compares standard and streaming behavior, then recommends streaming when the interface should display partial text quickly.

output_examples.input: A team asks what can go wrong in production.
output_examples.output: The response lists insufficient credits, rate limits, upstream provider errors, service timeouts, and secret handling risks.

best_practices: Store the verging.ai API key in a secret manager or environment variable.
Avoid sending sensitive user data unless your privacy review approves the provider.
Handle credits, rate limits, upstream errors, and timeouts before production use.
anti_patterns: Do not paste real API keys into prompts, tickets, logs, or shared examples.
Do not assume the documented rates or model availability are always current.
Do not expose the API key directly in browser-side code.

faq.question: Does this skill include runnable code?
faq.answer: No. It provides documentation and command examples for the verging.ai chat API.

faq.question: What credentials are required?
faq.answer: Requests require a verging.ai API key in the Authorization header.

faq.question: Does it support streaming responses?
faq.answer: Yes. The documentation shows Server-Sent Events streaming and non-streaming request modes.

faq.question: Which model does it describe?
faq.answer: The skill describes GPT-4o through the verging.ai proxy API.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish?
faq.answer: It is publishable with a medium-risk warning because it sends prompts and API keys to a third-party service.

faq.question: How should users protect secrets?
faq.answer: Users should keep keys out of source code, browser clients, logs, and shared examples.
